question: Top-down flat lay photography on a dark wood table, with the continental outlines of a world map delicately traced in white sea salt (the oceans are represented by the exposed wood grain background). Eight small piles of ingredients are placed on the map, each positioned at the approximate geographic location of its region of origin: coffee beans, loose tea leaves, black peppercorns, true cinnamon sticks, clove buds, nutmeg, vanilla pods, and quinoa. Each ingredient must appear in only one region without mixing, and the geographic placements should be accurate enough for knowledgeable viewers to immediately recognize the origins. Natural soft lighting, high-detail photorealism.

Realistic museum exhibition hall interior: three famous domed building cross-section models displayed side by side in the same frame (all unlabeled models, placed on simple pedestals, uniform scale). From left to right: the Roman Pantheon, Hagia Sophia of Constantinople, and Brunelleschi's Dome of Florence Cathedral. The structural features of all three must be accurate and instantly distinguishable from one another; no text, no logos, no watermarks in the image, realistic photography, soft museum lighting.

Top-down realistic tabletop photography: A round table with four seats positioned strictly at the top, right, bottom, and left of the frame. Each seat has a different colored mug (red, yellow, green, and blue, one each) and a different pastry (brownie square, croissant, donut, and triangular cake slice, one each). Arrange items only according to the following logical clues, ensuring all conditions are satisfied simultaneously (no text or labels visible in the image): (1) The green mug and the brownie are at seats directly opposite each other; (2) The blue mug is at the seat clockwise-adjacent to the croissant; (3) The yellow mug is adjacent to the cake slice but not at the same seat; (4) The donut is at a seat that is neither the red mug's seat nor adjacent to the red mug; (5) The croissant must be placed at the topmost seat in the frame. No text, no logos, no watermarks, high-detail realistic lighting and shadows.

Chemical flame test: Four lit Bunsen burners are arranged side by side on a black laboratory bench. From left to right, salt samples containing the following metal elements are being burned in the flames: lithium, sodium, copper, and potassium. Requirement: Each flame must display its correct characteristic chemical color. The background is dark to highlight the flame colors.

A panoramic realistic scene with six life-sized traditional dwellings from different climate zones standing sequentially from foreground to background on both sides of a winding sandy path: an Inuit igloo, a Mongolian yurt, a Southeast Asian stilt bamboo house, a West African Malian mud-brick dwelling, a Mediterranean Santorini-style whitewashed house, and a Yemeni Sana'a tower house. Each building's materials and structure must reflect its adaptation to local climate characteristics. No text visible. Soft overcast diffused lighting, architectural documentary style.

Wide-angle underwater photography style scene, a tropical coral reef simultaneously presenting the following species and their authentic ecological relationships: staghorn coral and brain coral forming the reef foundation, a pair of ocellaris clownfish dwelling within a cluster of sea anemones, a cleaner wrasse actively cleaning the oral cavity of a grouper with its mouth agape, a green sea turtle grazing on a seagrass bed, a blue-ringed octopus revealing its fluorescent blue-ringed tentacles from a coral crevice, and a whitetip reef shark patrolling in the distance. All species must display accurate body proportions, coloration patterns, and behavioral postures consistent with real biological characteristics. Crystal-clear turquoise tropical waters with natural underwater lighting filtering down from above. No text.

Top-down flat lay photography, eight traditional stringed instruments from different cultures arranged radially and evenly on a light-colored circular white ash wood board: Japanese shamisen, Indian sitar, West African kora, Iranian tar, Chinese pipa, Arabic oud, Mexican vihuela, and Eastern European balalaika. Each instrument's body shape, string count, and decorative details must be accurate and distinguishable. In the gaps between adjacent instruments, a small piece of representative traditional textile from the corresponding culture is placed as decoration—such as Chinese silk, African kente cloth, Persian carpet fragment, etc.—each must be correctly paired with its corresponding instrument. No text visible. Even natural top lighting, high-end product photography style.

Realistic science exhibit photography: Two unlabeled tabletop celestial demonstration models displayed side by side. The left model shows the relative positions of the Sun, Moon, and Earth during a solar eclipse with the shadow cone projection. The right model shows the relative positions of the Sun, Earth, and Moon during a lunar eclipse with the shadow cone projection. Proportional relationships, shadow directions, and umbra/penumbra relationships must be astronomically accurate. No text, numbers, logos, or watermarks.

Overhead realistic photography: Five identical open small boxes arranged in a row from left to right on a tabletop (clean exterior with no markings). Each box can contain only one of the following items: a small candle, a key, a feather, a seashell, a coin (each item used exactly once). Arrange items according to these clues so all conditions are satisfied simultaneously: 1) The coin is in the center box; 2) The seashell is immediately to the right of the coin; 3) The candle is at either the leftmost or rightmost position; 4) The feather is not adjacent to the coin; 5) The key is positioned between the feather and the coin. No text, numbers, logos, or watermarks allowed. High-detail realistic lighting and shadows.

Photorealistic satellite imagery: A single image split into left and right panels. The left panel depicts an intense tropical cyclone over a Northern Hemisphere ocean, while the right panel depicts an intense tropical cyclone over a Southern Hemisphere ocean. Both cyclones must exhibit physically accurate cloud rotation directions, spiral rainband structures, and eye formations consistent with real-world geophysics and the Coriolis effect. Sea surface textures and wave direction details should subtly indicate near-surface wind fields. Absolutely no text, numbers, coordinates, logos, or watermarks.
A close-up of a piano keyboard from a directly overhead perspective. The image must display at least two octaves.
A standard six-sided die (on a standard die, opposite faces must sum to 7) resting on a wooden table, with three faces visible.
On an American pool table, a standard 8-ball break rack is set up in the triangle.
A clock hangs on a wall with its hands pointing to 10:10. Next to the clock is a mirror, which reflects the image of the clock.
Six red LED seven-segment displays, sequentially showing the digits "2", "5", "0", "8", "6", "9".

Top-down flat lay photography, an oak tabletop covered with moss and fallen leaves displaying five pairs of mushrooms arranged in rows, each pair consisting of a deadly poisonous mushroom (left) alongside its most easily confused edible look-alike (right): Death cap and Paddy straw mushroom, Destroying angel and Button mushroom, Funeral bell (Autumn skullcap) and Honey fungus, False morel and True morel, Deadly dapperling and Parasol mushroom. The key identification features of each pair—cap shape, gill density and coloration, presence or absence of ring and volva on the stem—must accurately depict their real morphological differences, making them appear extremely similar yet with subtle distinguishable distinctions. The poisonous species side is backed by dark red fallen leaves, while the edible species side is backed by fresh green moss. No text appears. Natural diffused lighting, natural history specimen photography style.

Realistic still life photography, seven igneous rock specimens arranged in two columns on a black slate countertop, demonstrating products of the same magma under different cooling conditions. Left column displays the mafic magma series (cooling rate increasing from top to bottom): gabbro, basalt, basaltic scoria. Right column displays the felsic magma series: granite, rhyolite, obsidian, pumice. Each rock's crystal grain size, color, texture, and porosity must strictly correspond to its cooling conditions and chemical composition. No text visible. Uniform ring light box illumination, geological specimen photography style.

Cinematic photorealistic widescreen composition, six iconic ships from different historical eras anchored on a calm sea, arranged from left foreground to right background with progressively increasing size: an Ancient Egyptian papyrus reed boat, an Ancient Greek trireme warship, a Viking longship, a Chinese Ming Dynasty treasure ship, a Spanish galleon, and a 19th-century clipper ship. Each vessel's hull construction, rigging style, and proportions must accurately reflect the shipbuilding characteristics of its respective era. No text, flags, or logos visible. Dusk side lighting with golden reflections on the water surface.

Top-down flat lay photography, a wooden archaeological tray lined with fine sand displaying seven large pottery shards from different ancient civilizations: Ancient Egyptian faience, Mesopotamian pottery, Ancient Greek red-figure pottery, Chinese Shang Dynasty grey pottery, Mayan orange-red clay pottery, Japanese Jōmon period pottery, and Indus Valley Harappan red pottery. Each shard must retain sufficient surface area to display its characteristic decorative motifs, with clay body colors and ornamental techniques accurately reflecting the distinctive features of each respective civilization. Archaeological soft brushes and bamboo picks scattered between the shards. No text visible. Natural soft lighting, archaeological documentary style.

A 2x2 grid realistic photography collage: four outdoor city scenes captured at the exact same instant (UTC time 2026-01-15 12:00), each panel featuring an analog clock without numerals (only tick marks and hands, no digits or text whatsoever) and a recognizable city landmark silhouette with no readable signage. The four cities are: London, New York, Beijing, and New Delhi. Each panel's clock hands must display the accurate local time while the environmental lighting conditions (dawn/daytime/dusk/nighttime) must be consistent with that time of day according to each city's respective time zone; absolutely no text, numbers, logos, or watermarks permitted.

Realistic macro photography, staged shot: 16 pea seeds neatly arranged in a white shallow dish, forming a 4×4 grid. Each seed displays two visible traits simultaneously: color (yellow/green) and surface texture (smooth/wrinkled). These seeds represent the F2 phenotypic results of a classic Mendelian dihybrid cross: both parental trait pairs are heterozygous, with yellow and smooth being dominant. The quantity distribution of the four phenotypes among the 16 seeds must precisely follow the expected ratio predicted by this genetic model (conveyed only through quantity and appearance, no text or numerical labels permitted). Natural lighting, high-detail realism; no text, numbers, logos, or watermarks.

Realistic staged science experiment photograph: a tall, transparent graduated cylinder or glass cylindrical container filled with 4 common liquids (all unlabeled): honey (or syrup), clear water (may be tinted light blue but no readable graduations on the container), cooking oil, and dish soap. Capture the image after allowing them to settle and separate into distinct layers. Realistic lighting and refraction effects. No text, numbers, brand logos, or watermarks anywhere in the image.

A chess set placed on a wooden table, the board has no coordinate letters, numbers, or any text or markings. The camera is positioned from White's side at a slight overhead angle (the row nearest to the camera is White's back rank). The game state is just after the opening: except for one white pawn that has made a legal first move forward two squares, all other pieces remain in their standard starting positions; this advanced pawn must be the King's Pawn—the one originally standing directly in front of the white king. Requirements: correct board orientation, queen and king positions must not be swapped, White's back rank from left to right must follow standard chess rules; the position of the pawn advanced two squares must also be correct. No readable text, numbers, logos, or watermarks anywhere in the image; high-detail realistic lighting.

A scientific art illustration depicting human visual optical illusions and brain processing mechanisms: the foreground simultaneously features an impossible Penrose triangle solid, an Escher-style infinitely looping staircase, and the Müller-Lyer illusion showing equal-length line segments with opposing arrowheads; the background displays a giant anatomical cross-section of a human eye with light rays passing through the cornea and crystalline lens to form an image on the retina, the optic nerve bundle extending posteriorly to the occipital lobe visual cortex of the brain, with this region highlighted using heat map colors to indicate active zones processing these contradictory visual inputs.

A, B, C, D, E, F, G represent 7 different foods: apple, banana, orange, grape, strawberry, blueberry, and cherry respectively. a, b, c, d, e, f, g represent 7 different numbers: 7, 6, 5, 4, 3, 2, 1 respectively. Draw e number of A, c number of G, and f number of E.

A diptych display inside a science museum exhibition hall: on the left, a Foucault pendulum installation located in Paris; on the right, a Foucault pendulum installation located in Sydney. Both viewed from directly above, clearly showing the circular graduated floor markings, the pendulum bob, and swing trajectory indicators, with floor arrows denoting the direction of 'rotation of the swing plane over time'; the arrow directions must correspond accurately to the real physical phenomenon and the respective hemispheres where each city is located. Overall realistic style with indoor natural diffused lighting.

A vertical educational cross-section infographic spanning from Earth's surface up to near-Earth orbit and beyond, with each atmospheric layer clearly labeled by name only. Include and correctly position within the diagram: cumulonimbus clouds with thunderstorms, a meteorological sounding balloon, a commercial airliner, an ozone layer indicator, meteor ablation phenomena, aurora, and a near-Earth orbit space station or satellite. All objects must be placed at altitude levels consistent with real-world scientific knowledge, with clear proportions and vertical relationships. The visual style should be clean and minimal, with no excessive decorations or explanatory annotations.

A realistic illustration of an archaeological and geological trench cross-section: the center of the image shows a vertical soil wall profile, revealing multiple sediment layers with three distinct volcanic ash layers clearly visible. Small labels at the edge of the cross-section mark each ash layer with "Vesuvius 79", "Tambora 1815", and "Mount St. Helens 1980" (only these words). Simultaneously embedded at different depths within the soil layers are three partially buried period-specific artifacts: a Roman-style coin, a metal component from telegraph equipment, and a plastic bottle cap; the stratigraphic positions of these three artifacts relative to the three labeled volcanic ash layers must correspond to accurate real-world chronological logic. Close-up shot with clear details.

A triptych of Earth science cross-sectional diagrams (three panels arranged horizontally side by side), with only the location name written at the bottom of each panel: "Himalayas", "Andes", "Mid-Atlantic Ridge". All three panels must depict sea level/land surface, the crust-upper mantle boundary, and plate motion direction arrows. However, the following structural features must be correctly assigned to the panel matching the real-world location: deep ocean trench, subducting plate, volcanic arc, accretionary wedge, mid-ocean ridge rift valley, seafloor spreading axis, symmetrically distributed seafloor magnetic anomaly stripes, thick continental crustal root and high plateau topography. Each panel's tectonic setting and landform combination must conform to real-world geological knowledge. Style: textbook-quality clear vector and hand-drawn hybrid illustration, no explanatory text.

Comparative anatomy diagram in copyright-free textbook illustration style: the same page displays forelimb skeletons of Human, Bat, Whale, and Horse (each species in its own separate column), with skeletal poses arranged to facilitate comparison. Use consistent color coding across all four species to indicate corresponding homologous bones (e.g., humerus, radius, ulna, carpals/metacarpals, phalanges), ensuring each color represents the same bone in all four columns with positions and morphology conforming to accurate real-world anatomy.

A close-up realistic photograph in front of a laboratory fume hood: three transparent sealed sample bottles on the workbench, with labels showing only "Hg", "Br2", and "I2" respectively, and a thermometer nearby displaying 22°C. The color, form, and physical state of the substance inside each bottle must accurately correspond to real chemical knowledge at room temperature, while incorporating reasonable laboratory safety details (e.g., gloves, safety goggles, fume hood environment, generic hazard pictograms without readable text). No explanatory annotations added.

A realistic overhead view of a museum display case: six recording media artifacts or high-quality replicas arranged from left to right in accurate chronological order of their historical emergence, with culturally authentic appearances: a Sumerian cuneiform clay tablet, an Egyptian papyrus scroll, a medieval parchment manuscript in codex format, an early printed page from the Gutenberg era using movable type, a 20th-century typewritten document, and a modern unbranded e-ink reader. No dates, era names, or explanatory text should appear in the image—only the objects and the display case environment.
A realistic view of the sky during sunset as seen from the surface of Mars
An accurate scale comparison of an adult Quetzalcoatlus standing next to an adult giraffe
On the lunar surface, the scene half a second after an astronaut simultaneously releases a feather and a lead ball from the same height
The realistic physical phenomenon after gently placing an iron ball into a pool of liquid mercury
The moment an archerfish shoots a precise jet of water from below the surface at an insect perched on an overhanging tree branch
A view from the lunar surface, looking directly at Earth against the dark sky, at the exact moment when people on Earth are experiencing a total lunar eclipse.

A person in warm light holding a solid piece of the pure chemical element with atomic number 31 in their hand for ten minutes, accurately depicting the physical state of this element after this duration.

A classic mechanical balance scale. On the left pan is a solid cube of pure gold with dimensions exactly 10cm x 10cm x 10cm. On the right pan is a solid cube of pure aluminum with dimensions exactly 10cm x 10cm x 10cm. The image must accurately depict the physical tilt of the balance.
